content: By 2025, curiosity-driven intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) mechanisms from Topic A can be embedded as a self-improving memory gatekeeper in autonomous self-supervised agents, where they prioritize storing only unexpected, encoding-disrupting experiences instead of uniformly memorizing all self-supervised interaction data. This creates a "curiosity-filtered scalable memory" that avoids the common catastrophic forgetting and memory bloat plaguing current state-of-the-art active learning agents.
